documentation.file_docstring: Nix mode detection: When OPENCLAW_NIX_MODE=1, the gateway is running under Nix. In this mode: - No auto-install flows should be attempted - Missing dependencies should produce actionable Nix-specific error messages - Config is managed externally (read-only from Nix perspective)
inline_comments: Support historical (and occasionally misspelled) legacy state dirs.
State directory for mutable data (sessions, logs, caches). Can be overridden via OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR. Default: ~/.openclaw
Config file path (JSON5). Can be overridden via OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH. Default: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (or $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json)
Resolve the active config path by preferring existing config candidates before falling back to the canonical path.
Active config path (prefers existing config files).
Resolve default config path candidates across default locations. Order: explicit config path → state-dir-derived paths → new default.
Gateway lock directory (ephemeral). Default: os.tmpdir()/openclaw-<uid> (uid suffix when available).
OAuth credentials storage directory. Precedence: - `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_DIR` (explicit override) - `$*_STATE_DIR/credentials` (canonical server/default)

functions.doc: Resolve default config path candidates across default locations.   Order: explicit config path → state-dir-derived paths

concerns: Multiple concerns detected: business_logic, auth, logging
